description_long: This is a vintage-style briefcase made primarily of weathered, olive-green canvas with brown leather trim along the edges. The main compartment is accessed via a large flap that folds over the front and is secured by two leather straps with metal buckles. The interior is divided into several pockets, some of which contain visible hand tools, suggesting it may be used for carrying equipment or documents. The sides of the briefcase are soft and flexible, with no rigid frame, and the stitching and material show signs of wear and use. There are no visible brand markings, external pockets, or corner reinforcements, giving it a utilitarian and rugged appearance.
description: A weathered olive-green canvas briefcase with brown leather trim, flap closure, and interior pockets holding tools. The design is utilitarian, with no visible logos or external pockets.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open flap, interior pockets, and tools inside the briefcase.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the briefcase and the flexible, soft-sided construction.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned back panel with no external pockets or markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the layered compartments and the absence of rigid structure.
attribute_handle_type: A single flexible leather strap handle attached to the top with metal rings; the handle is fixed and not retractable.
attribute_lock_mechanism: Two leather straps with metal buckles located on the front flap; no key or combination lock present.
